methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B drone and autonomous systems company founded by Bobby Healy that has raised $110M to date. The Drones & Autonomous sector comprises 8 private peers including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation was conducted using the Stage-Progression method, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $110M total raised proxy, reflecting typical capital accretion from Series B to subsequent rounds. This method is applicable here and yields $209M. A Peer-Median approach was not viable because the narrow peer set lacked sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (only 1 same-stage peer identified across 5 total sector peers), making statistical triangulation unreliable without risking distortion from broader-sector medians.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $178M to $240M, with a median point of $209M. This range reflects reasonable downside (20% haircut) and upside (15% lift) around the Stage-Progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market anchors and limited visibility into comparable Series B transaction pricing within the sector. The valuation is sensitive to near-term capital deployment pace and operational milestones that would typically justify stage-up multiples in this space.
